Action: Wallet creates a wallet entry and collects credential offer via deeplink; Business Issuer creates credential offer with signed metadata enabled (ENABLE_SIGNED_METADATA=true)
Data:
- Credential type: unbound_example_sd_jwt
- Signed metadata feature enabled on issuer
- Credential offer deeplink
Expected Result: Wallet successfully validates and receives deeplink; Issuer returns credential offer

Action: Wallet retrieves issuer well-known configuration and collects token via pre-authorized code flow
Data:
- Well-known metadata endpoint
- Pre-authorized code
- Token endpoint request
Expected Result: Wallet successfully retrieves issuer configuration and exchanges pre-authorized code for token

Action: Wallet requests signed metadata from issuer using Accept: application/jwt header
Data:
- Token (JWT)
- GET request to issuer metadata endpoint
- Accept: application/jwt header
Expected Result: Issuer returns HTTP 200 with signed metadata as JWT (header.payload.signature)

Action: Wallet decodes JWT payload, validates JWT structure, and confirms required OID4VCI metadata claims are present
Data:
- Signed JWT response
- JWT payload containing OID4VCI claims
- Signature validation
Expected Result: Wallet successfully decodes payload; all required OID4VCI metadata fields are present and valid; signature is verified
